versionNotes: Sprint 4 W13 Three-Sprint Retrospective — no-AI form activity, 8 SDL dimension sections + examples + confirmation block
title: Three-Sprint Retrospective
description: Look back at Sprints 1-3 honestly. Where did you grow? Where did you barely engage? You'll use what you find here to choose your Sprint 4 gap.

questions.infoBlock:
Dimension 1 of 8 · Goals

Did you set clear goals for yourself in this course, or did you mostly do whatever the assignment asked?
questions.prompt: Fill in all three fields below. Be specific — vague claims without evidence tell you nothing about what you actually learned.
questions.placeholder:
What I'd claim: If I had to claim I grew in this dimension, I'd say ______.

Evidence I actually have: Specific assignment / artifact / moment I could point to: ______. (Or: I don't have any.)

Honest rating (pick one): Strong evidence / Weak evidence / No evidence / Didn't engage with this

questions.infoBlock:
Three example areas you might want to grow in

These are examples, not a menu. They show what a real Sprint 4 gap can look like. Expand any that sound relevant. You'll name your own gap in the Gap Statement activity — this is just priming.

Example 1: Learning to use AI to automate things

This is a real skill that matters professionally. But the gap underneath isn't "learn the AI tool" — it's the judgment to know when manual work is the wrong way to spend your attention, and what value YOU bring when AI handles the repetition. The technical skill is the vehicle. The metacognitive habit of noticing what you should and shouldn't delegate is the gap. If this excites you, your Sprint 4 gap might be "developing the judgment to know when AI delegation costs me something I needed to learn."

Example 2: Getting better at receiving feedback without defending

Most people respond to criticism by explaining themselves. The actual skill is staying open long enough to hear what's being offered before deciding whether to push back. This is a collaboration + metacognition gap. If this resonates, your Sprint 4 gap might be "noticing my defensive reflex in real-time and choosing curiosity instead."

Example 3: Understanding what kinds of work I find meaningful (vs just paying)

A lot of CS students pick the path that sounds employable and figure out what they actually want later. Doing the work to find out earlier is hard but it changes the next 5 years. This is a goals + metacognition gap. If this resonates, your Sprint 4 gap might be "developing a clearer, evidence-based answer to what kind of work I want to be doing."
questions.prompt: After expanding the examples above: does any direction resonate? You don't have to commit to anything here — this is priming for the Gap Statement. Jot a sentence or two about what (if anything) spoke to you, or write "none of these — my gap is somewhere else" and that's fine.
questions.placeholder: Example: The "receiving feedback without defending" one hit me — I caught myself explaining away the Sprint 2 peer eval feedback instead of sitting with it.

questions.infoBlock:
Before you export: confirm you understand

Read each item. You're acknowledging these by submitting your markdown export.

  1. I understand Sprint 4 is about finding ONE real gap and closing it with evidence.
  2. I understand the final defense is a 3-minute presentation in front of the class plus 2 minutes of Q&A.
  3. I understand the extra credit look-back assignments will be available to recover up to 50% of points I lost.
  4. I will start a Sprint 4 Google Doc, paste my markdown summary into it, and submit only PDF exports of that doc going forward.
questions.prompt: First, type "I confirm all four" to acknowledge the four items above. Then, in one or two sentences: what's the most uncomfortable thing this retrospective is showing you?
questions.placeholder:
I confirm all four.

The most uncomfortable thing: ______
